---
title: "CO2-FT Fe3O4 Microkinetics Course"
---

# Course Home

This Quarto book is a working course for connecting CO2 Fischer-Tropsch chemistry, Fe3O4 surface reaction networks, and microkinetic modeling.

The first version focuses on a minimal RWGS-centered mechanism that can later be extended toward carburization, Fe carbide formation, and chain-growth chemistry.

## Learning Goals

- Explain why Fe3O4 and Fe carbide phases both matter in CO2-FT.
- Translate a proposed elementary-step network into rate equations.
- Check site balance and mass balance before interpreting model output.
- Use sensitivity analysis to separate controlling assumptions from cosmetic parameters.

## Repository Map

```text
lessons/      Quarto lesson chapters
mechanisms/   Mechanism definitions
src/          Python source code for model examples
tests/        Balance and regression checks
notebooks/    Exploratory notebooks
data/         Input data and parameter tables
figures/      Generated and manually prepared figures
```
